pi_items.source: IMO Resolution A.751(18) standard: tactical diameter must not exceed 5 ship lengths. Calculation: 5.0 * 225m (Lpp) = 1125.0m. Web search and official IMO documentation verified.
IMO Resolution A.751(18) standard: advance must not exceed 4.5 ship lengths. Calculation: 4.5 * 225m (Lpp) = 1012.5m. Web search and official IMO documentation verified.

IMO Resolution A.751(18) standard: track reach in full astern stopping test must not exceed 15 ship lengths. Calculation: 15 * 225m (Lpp) = 3375.0m. Web search and official IMO documentation verified.
Vessel specification from task description. Consistent across metadata (lpp=225) and description (Length Lpp: 225 meters). 225m is a plausible length for a large cargo/container vessel.
Vessel specification from task description. Consistent across metadata (target_speed=14.0) and description (Service Speed: 14.0 knots). 14.0 knots is a plausible service speed for a 225m ship.

pi_items.metadata_value: ["a) Turning Circle Test", "b) Zig-Zag Test", "c) Crash Stop Test"]
pi_items.verified_value: ["a) Turning Circle Test", "b) Zig-Zag Test", "c) Crash Stop Test"]
pi_items.source: All three test names are standard IMO maneuverability test procedures per IMO Resolution A.751(18): (1) Turning Circle Test evaluates turning ability, (2) Zig-Zag Test evaluates course-keeping ability, (3) Crash Stop Test evaluates stopping ability.

pi_items.verified_value: IMO Resolution A.751(18) - Interim Standards for Ship Manoeuvrability
pi_items.source: Web search retrieved official IMO standards document and text version confirming all three maneuverability criteria multipliers are correct per international maritime standards.
Task specification value. This is a scenario-specific constraint (South of 50.65°N) to place trials in open water. Not based on IMO standards but represents reasonable nautical coordinates.

pi_items.metadata_value: Bridge Command (referenced in paths and configuration)
pi_items.verified_value: Bridge Command is a real, open-source maritime simulator (GPL v2) by James Packer
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed Bridge Command (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/) is a legitimate free interactive 3D ship and radar simulator for Windows, macOS, and Linux used for navigation, radar, and ship handling training.

privileged_info_summary: The task involves setting up IMO maneuverability trial scenarios for a fictional vessel 'MV Blue Whale' (225m length, 14.0 knots service speed) using Bridge Command maritime simulator. All calculated maneuverability criteria have been verified against official IMO Resolution A.751(18): tactical diameter of 1125.0m = 5.0 × Lpp, advance of 1012.5m = 4.5 × Lpp, and stopping distance of 3375.0m = 15 × Lpp. The three required test scenarios (Turning Circle, Zig-Zag, and Crash Stop) are authentic IMO standard tests. Bridge Command is confirmed as a legitimate open-source maritime simulator. Data is synthetic (simulation setup task), not based on external datasets.
